title: Week 7 Discussion — Misinformation & The Onion
html:
cs ethics discussion analyzing a satirical the onion piece ("Alabama Supreme Court Rules Frozen Burritos Are Children") as a case study in misinformation and source-checking discipline.

TL;DR

  • the onion satire used as the artifact — its structural resemblance to real news is what makes it dangerous when mis-shared.
  • Source-vetting checklist: publication reputation, cross-referencing, language tone (absolutist vs nuanced), author bias.
  • Frames information literacy as civic responsibility in the viral-misinformation era.
  • Sits in the same cs ethics term as the wikipedia crowdsourcing and pay for privacy essays — Yahir's privacy/truth axis.

cs ethics · the onion · misinformation · information literacy · oregon state
